title: transaction — subscribed events
description: Transaction is a CONSUMER of invoice / payment / refund / chargeback / coupon / tax module events — every business-level money movement in the platform triggers a listener here that records the movement via TransactionRecorder. Also consumes tenancy lifecycle events (provisioning + suspension + erasure) via the tenancy hook chain.

listeners.purpose: The primary revenue-recording path. Maps invoice.paid → RecordInvoicePaidTransactionJob. Records a transaction (kind='invoice_payment', reference_type='Invoice', reference_id=invoice.id) with [debit: cash, credit: accounts_receivable] ledger entries. Runs afterCommit on the invoice update; if the invoice-paid state rolls back, this listener never fires.

Wave 4.5 bridge. Maps refund.issued → RecordRefundIssuedTransactionJob. Records TWO transactions: (kind='refund_issued') [debit: refunds_owed, credit: cash] AND (kind='adjustment', reversal_reason='refund') [debit: refunds_expense, credit: revenue]. Both share source_transaction_id pointing at the original invoice-payment transaction.

Wave 4.5 bridge. Maps chargeback.filed → RecordChargebackTransactionJob. Records a transaction (kind='chargeback_debit', reversal_reason='chargeback') with [debit: chargebacks_expense, credit: cash] ledger entries.

Maps coupon.redeemed → recording of coupon credit. TransactionRecorder::recordCouponCredit(coupon_redemption) creates a transaction (kind='coupon_credit', reference_type='CouponRedemption') with [debit: coupons_expense, credit: revenue] entries — the discount is recognized as a revenue reduction + a marketing/coupons expense.

Maps tax.remitted (when the tenant files + pays tax to a regulator) → TransactionRecorder::recordTaxRemittance(tax_remittance) creates a transaction (kind='tax_remittance') with [debit: tax_payable, credit: cash] entries — tax_payable is cleared as the money flows to the regulator.

Wave 4.5 bridge. When payment.captured carries a gateway_fee_cents > 0 (Stripe fees, Paddle fees, etc.), records a separate transaction (kind='fee_charge') with [debit: gateway_fees, credit: cash] entries — the fee is recognized as an expense against cash. Distinct from the invoice payment recording — fee is a separate transaction with its own reference_type='Payment' + reference_id.

When an invoice is marked uncollectible (write-off), TransactionRecorder::recordWriteOff(invoice) creates a transaction (kind='write_off') with [debit: write_offs, credit: accounts_receivable] entries — the receivable is removed + the loss is recognized. Distinct from refund_issued (which reverses a paid transaction) — write_off recognizes an unpaid invoice as loss.
Records the opening-balance transaction for a newly-provisioned tenant. See hooks.json:RecordOpeningBalanceOnTenantProvisioned.

Highest-priority listener — runs BEFORE the FK CASCADE. Archives every transaction + ledger entry to compliance-storage as JSONL keyed by (tenant_id, effective_date). See hooks.json:ArchiveTransactionsToComplianceOnTenantErased.
Runs AFTER the archive listener completes. Cascade delete via FK CASCADE. See hooks.json:PurgeTransactionDataForErasedTenant.

Reacts to LedgerImbalanceDetected fires from the reconciler (via the compliance module's event ingest). Sets tenant.transaction_halt_new=true — new transactions REFUSED until an ops-authored fix lands. Every fire pages the on-call via the LedgerImbalanceDetectedNotification channel.

The reverse of HaltNewTransactionsOnImbalance. When ops files a compliance ticket resolution + the imbalance is cleared, sets tenant.transaction_halt_new=false. Writes a critical audit row + fires an ops-visible notification confirming the resume.
